is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Tanzanian children with P. falciparum malaria (mild and cerebral malaria defined by P. falciparum parasitemia).

reports_target_markers.evidence: The paper studies human host genetic polymorphisms (NOS2 promoter G-954C and CCTTT microsatellite), not any P. falciparum drug-resistance markers such as Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfk13, etc.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Frequencies reported are for human NOS2 genotypes, not parasite drug-resistance loci.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples collected at Muhimbili Medical Center in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania — a sub-national site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Recruited May 1994 to January 1995, a window under 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from Tanzanian children; American samples are human host controls, not parasites.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study of a Tanzanian cohort.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Cohort described elsewhere (Anstey et al. J Exp Med 1996) — 191 Tanzanian children; this analyzes 180-185 of them, subset of a prior collection.

supplement.evidence: No parasite drug-resistance marker data exists in this human-genetics study; supplement would not change eligibility.

assessment.summary: This study examines human host NOS2 promoter polymorphisms (G-954C SNP and CCTTT microsatellite) in Tanzanian children with malaria and their association with disease severity and nitric oxide production. It reports no P. falciparum drug-resistance markers, so it is ineligible.
exclusion_reasons: Does not report any curated P. falciparum drug-resistance marker codon positions
Frequencies reported are for human host NOS2 genotypes, not parasite drug-resistance loci
